examples.title: Aegis / Cobalt Software - Q2 Planning
examples.summary: Lisa Park (CSM) met with Lauren Bishop to discuss the upcoming June renewal, address the March Detect outage, and review the recently released Comply v2. Lauren was candid about internal concerns raised by the six-hour outage and noted that procurement is requiring at least one competitive quote from SentinelShield. The team is planning to expand their cloud workload footprint by about 40 workloads through Q3, which will factor into renewal pricing options. Lisa committed to sending the post-incident review, preparing two renewal proposal options, and scheduling a compliance walkthrough for June.

examples.title: Aegis / Quantum Edge - Renewal Concerns
examples.summary: Maria Santos met with Brianna Foster and Leo Marquez to address a serious six-hour outage on March 10-11 that caused a loss of visibility in Aegis Detect, breaching their 99.5% uptime SLA. The customer expressed eroded confidence in platform reliability and revealed they are actively evaluating a competitor, SentinelShield, which is offering aggressive pricing and a 99.9% SLA. Additional concerns included platform stagnation, upcoming compliance needs (SOC 2, HIPAA), and budget constraints going into renewal. Maria outlined next steps including a renewal proposal with an SLA credit, pricing adjustments, access to the upcoming Comply v2 product, and an engineering call to address Leo's infrastructure concerns.

examples.title: Competitive Threat Assessment - Post Outage
examples.summary: The team convened to address fallout from a six-hour platform outage that caused a complete loss of threat monitoring visibility. Competitors SentinelShield and CyberNova are actively targeting at-risk accounts, including Hartfield Financial and prospect Clearvale Systems. The group aligned on a two-part response strategy: leading with a transparent post-mortem and remediation narrative now, followed by a Comply v2 launch touchpoint in early April to show forward momentum. Key concerns include a potential liability issue involving a customer who claims an actual security incident occurred during the outage window.

examples.title: Aegis / Trailhead Marketplace - Renewal Confirmation
examples.summary: Rachel Torres and Chris Lee held a renewal call with Isaac Brennan of Trailhead, covering fourteen months of experience with Protect and Detect. Isaac acknowledged a mid-March six-hour outage as a concern but praised the thorough post-mortem and the subsequent technical fixes. The team discussed adding Aegis Comply v2 at a promotional rate of $18,500 annually (locked for two years), which Isaac found compelling given upcoming PCI audit cycles. Isaac indicated strong internal support for the renewal and agreed on next steps including a procurement one-pager, contract execution by end of April, and a technical onboarding call post-signing.

examples.title: Aegis / Axiom Labs - Multi-Year Renewal
examples.summary: Aisha Johnson (CSM) held a renewal planning call with Ryan Caldwell and Mina Joshi ahead of their end-of-May contract anniversary. The customer expressed strong overall satisfaction with the platform, particularly with the Identity and Comply v2 modules, while acknowledging the March Detect outage was serious but handled well. Aisha presented a three-year renewal proposal featuring an elastic seat model and enterprise API tier, and the conversation surfaced a previously unknown streaming endpoint that could solve a latency issue in the customer's internal alerting pipeline. The call concluded positively with clear next steps including a formal proposal, a technical deep dive, and looping in finance and legal for final approval.

examples.title: Detect Outage - Remediation Plan Review
examples.summary: Megan, Raj, and Brian convened on day three of an ongoing outage affecting the Detect product's event processing pipeline. Raj explained that a single point of failure in the event ingestion layer caused a cascading failure, leaving customers with zero threat monitoring visibility for approximately six hours. The remediation plan includes deploying redundant active-active processing nodes (live by end of day) and implementing a circuit breaker pattern (by Wednesday evening), with full tenant visibility restored in a phased rollout by tomorrow morning. The team aligned on communication protocols, retroactive security analysis, and a postmortem timeline.

examples.title: Aegis / Meridian Capital - Service Reliability Discussion
examples.summary: Suki Nakamura from a financial services firm expressed serious concern over a six-hour threat monitoring outage on Monday that left her security team blind during ongoing regulatory audit conversations. Ravi Gupta acknowledged a known architectural single point of failure in Aegis Detect's event processing pipeline and outlined remediation steps including redundant nodes and a circuit breaker pattern targeted for end-of-month deployment. Suki disclosed that her CISO has initiated a formal vendor review and that the company is actively evaluating competitor SentinelShield. The call concluded with concrete commitments: Ravi will send an infrastructure audit summary by Monday, the technical fix will ship by end of March, and a follow-up check-in call is scheduled for two weeks out.

examples.title: Detect Outage - Customer Impact Assessment
examples.summary: The team convened an urgent internal call to address a significant outage of their Detect product that left customers without security monitoring for approximately six hours overnight. A single point of failure in the event ingestion pipeline caused a cascading failure, and critically, the company's own internal alerting also failed because the health checks were tied to the same broken pipeline — meaning a customer, not the team, flagged the outage. At least 47 support tickets have been logged, key enterprise accounts including Fortex and Meridian Financial are expressing dissatisfaction and referencing competitors, and SLA compliance issues are expected to result in credits. The team aligned on immediate action items including drafting a formal executive incident communication, pulling the full SLA exposure list, looping in legal and finance, and preparing customer-facing talking points to clarify that the Comply product was unaffected.

examples.title: URGENT: Blackridge Investments - Complete Loss of Threat Visibility
examples.summary: Julia Tran from Blackridge Investments called Aegis Cloud Security support in a critical situation where her firm has had zero threat visibility for over three hours due to a known event processing pipeline failure. Marcus Williams confirmed the outage is a cascading failure affecting multiple customers on the same processing cluster, with engineering estimating restoration within two more hours. Julia expressed serious frustration over the lack of proactive communication, a single point of failure in the architecture, and the operational impact of having analysts manually reviewing logs. She disclosed they have been evaluating a competitor (SentinelShield) and has a board meeting next week where she must explain the security blind spot. Marcus committed to a formal incident report, SLA review, AE loop-in, and a direct phone call upon service restoration.

examples.title: Support Case #5889 - Ridgeline Logistics Detect Latency Issues
examples.summary: Priya Patel from Aegis support contacted Paula Schneider of Ridgeline (a manufacturing/logistics company) regarding a multi-day alert latency issue with the Aegis Detect product. Priya confirmed the problem was caused by a cascading failure in Aegis's event processing pipeline, not anything on the customer's side, and apologized for the lack of proactive communication. Paula expressed concern about the security gap created by hour-long alert delays across their OT and manufacturing environments, and noted potential SLA violations and cyber insurance reporting requirements. Priya committed to a two-hour written update with fix timeline and backfill status, and offered to involve the account manager for remediation and service credit discussions.

examples.title: Support Case #9279 - Summit Trust Billing Inquiry
examples.summary: Gregory Fisk, IT Director at Summit Trust, called regarding a billing discrepancy on his January invoice showing 1,400 seats for Aegis Identity when their contract specifies 1,200 seats. Sarah Chen investigated and traced the overage to approximately 200 test accounts created during a December migration that were never deprovisioned. Sarah agreed to submit a billing adjustment request to the finance team, which will take 5-7 business days to review. Gregory also received guidance on bulk deprovisioning test accounts and setting up usage threshold alerts to prevent recurrence.

examples.title: Aegis / Atlas Precision - Contract Discussion
examples.summary: Rachel Torres and Maria Santos from Aegis met with Wayne Keller of Atlas to discuss the upcoming end-of-March contract renewal and billing concerns. Wayne's finance team flagged overage charges on the Aegis Detect module for January and February, which were caused by the addition of two new production facilities expanding event volume beyond the 500 million monthly cap. Rachel proposed upgrading to a 750 million event tier at an additional $310/month, which would likely be more cost-effective than continued overages. Wayne also expressed interest in exploring the Aegis Comply module for SOC 2 reporting, and both sides agreed on an annual renewal with a mid-March internal target deadline.

examples.title: Aegis / Harborview Banking - Threat Detection Tuning
examples.summary: Lisa Park and Kevin O'Brien from Aegis held a threat detection tuning session with Patricia Dunn (VP of Security) and Michael Russo (Compliance Officer) from Harborview Banking, who went live on Aegis Detect seven weeks ago. The primary concerns discussed were alert fatigue from false positives driven by geo-anomaly detection for multi-timezone staff, compliance coverage gaps for FFIEC/SOX/GLBA requirements, and inefficient alert routing to a single shared SOC inbox. Kevin enabled alert mode on the privileged access monitoring ruleset during the call and identified that the data retention setting needs to be updated from 90 days to at least 12 months. A follow-up working session was planned for the week of March 16th to configure trusted location profiles and work schedule profiles for the Manila team.

examples.title: Support Case #8179 - Clearwater Medical Overage Charges Dispute
examples.summary: Clearwater Medical's Ajay Mehta (Compliance Manager) and Natalie Simmons (IT Director) contacted Aegis Cloud Security to dispute a $4,000+ April invoice. The primary dispute involves $3,200 in Comply module overages tied to report generation during the v2 launch on April 7th, where Ajay explored multi-framework reporting features with no knowledge of plan limits and no in-app alerts — which turned out to be a misconfiguration affecting migrated accounts. An additional $840 Detect module overage appears potentially linked to replay traffic generated by Aegis's own March outage, which is being investigated. Sarah Chen committed to escalating both disputes as high priority, with a written Comply dispute response within 48 hours and a Detect log analysis by end of day.

examples.title: Support Case #7572 - Brightpath Commerce Invoice Discrepancy
examples.summary: Kendra Wallace, CTO of Brightpath Commerce, contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a $1,400 discrepancy on her February invoice. Marcus Williams identified an overage charge of 2.31 terabytes under the Aegis Protect module, which was traced to a backup policy change made on January 3rd that added five new workloads including database instances. The team clarified that billing is calculated on pre-compression raw data, meaning the policy expansion unintentionally pushed usage over the 10 TB contracted tier. Marcus agreed to document the findings in a case summary and connect Kendra with account manager Devon Park to discuss a potential billing adjustment.

examples.title: Weekly Engineering Standup
examples.summary: The engineering team held a sprint status check-in covering the Detect pipeline reliability work and the upcoming Comply v2 launch. Mike Romano reported being 60% through the event ingestion refactor, with the end-of-March deadline assessed at roughly 60-40 odds due to uncertainty around Kafka configuration and redundant node setup. Tyler Washington completed alert deduplication work and will assist by spiking on the Kafka investigation to help reduce risk. Tom Bradley will communicate to product that the March target stands but carries acknowledged technical uncertainty, while Chris Lee continues weekly syncs with Priya's team on the Comply v2 April launch.

examples.title: Support Case #3296 - Pinnacle Insurance Comply v2 Report Formatting Issue
examples.summary: Oliver Grant contacted Aegis Cloud Security support to report multiple formatting bugs in the newly launched Aegis Comply v2 PDF report export. The issues include section header text truncation on long control category names, a table pagination problem splitting the header row from its data, and incorrect page count in the footer. Marcus confirmed the bugs by reproducing them in a test environment and identified them as a rendering defect in the PDF generation service introduced with the v2 release. Marcus committed to escalating to engineering with high priority and providing an ETA via email the same day, given Oliver's internal audit review scheduled for the following week.

examples.title: Detect Team - Sprint Planning
examples.summary: The team held a sprint planning meeting to review carryover items and commit to work for the upcoming sprint. Nina flagged two edge cases in DETECT-219 related to wildcard regex matching that Tyler needs to fix before the ticket can be closed. Ananya raised an architectural concern about a single-node ingestion pipeline and proposed a design spike to address redundancy and failover, which Ravi agreed to scope collaboratively. The team settled on 13 story points for the sprint, including carryover, QA work, the design spike, and the alert deduplication ticket DETECT-231, intentionally leaving buffer after overcommitting last sprint.

examples.title: Comply v2 - GA Deployment Plan
examples.summary: The team held a pre-launch alignment call for the Comply v2 GA deployment scheduled for April 7th. Ravi confirmed infrastructure dry-runs in staging went cleanly, with a minor Lambda cold-start issue to be addressed via a warm-up ping. Nina reported full regression suite passage across all four compliance frameworks and three tenant sizes, flagging a p99 latency concern at 50 concurrent requests that will be monitored via a new MetricFlow alert. The team confirmed a phased rollout plan, a clean feature-flag-based rollback strategy, and agreed to keep the v1 reporting service running in parallel for two weeks post-GA.

examples.title: All Hands - April Update
examples.summary: The team held an April all-hands covering three main topics: a retrospective on the March Detect outage, the Comply v2 launch status, and Q2 roadmap planning. The Detect outage post-mortem revealed a single point of failure in the ingestion layer that caused a six-hour customer-facing blackout, with redundant nodes and a circuit breaker pattern now deployed but a chaos engineering validation still needed. Comply v2 launched on April 7th with multi-framework compliance reporting and is showing early positive adoption, though two open bugs remain around PDF formatting and concurrent report timeouts. For Q2, the team agreed to prioritize reliability and observability work on Detect over new features, deferring pure feature additions to Q3.

examples.title: Aegis / Redwood Clinical - ISO 27001 Preparation
examples.summary: Daniel Okafor and solutions architect Kevin O'Brien met with Thomas Hargrove of Redwood to introduce Comply v2 and discuss support for their ISO 27001 certification journey. Thomas shared that Redwood is in early stages of ISO prep, with a formal audit targeted for Q1 2027, and highlighted pain points around evidence collection and continuous monitoring. Kevin walked through Comply v2's on-demand reporting engine, which maps live environment data to Annex A controls and supports multi-framework coverage including HIPAA and SOC 2. The team agreed on a clear set of next steps: activating Comply v2 on Redwood's tenant, running a baseline report, and scheduling a technical working session with Thomas and his security analyst Priya.

examples.title: Comply v2 - Launch Readiness Review
examples.summary: Megan and Chris conducted a launch readiness review for Comply v2, targeting an April 7th release. A recent Detect outage pulled two engineers off the project for four days, causing a 5-6 day slip on the HIPAA and ISO 27001 reporting engine, which has formatting issues in staging. The API is in good shape with beta customers, but customer-facing documentation for HIPAA and ISO 27001 is still in first draft and poses the biggest launch risk. They aligned on key deadlines, escalation paths, and a follow-up check-in for the following Tuesday.

examples.title: Aegis / Stratos Cloud - Audit Preparation
examples.summary: Daniel Okafor and James Mitchell from Aegis met with Damien Rowe from Stratos to discuss using Aegis Comply to streamline their compliance preparation ahead of a Q2 (May) audit. Damien explained that their current process is highly manual, requiring engineers to spend weeks pulling evidence across spreadsheets for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance. The Aegis team walked through how Comply continuously maps cloud infrastructure to compliance frameworks and generates audit-ready evidence, noting integration with their existing Aegis Detect product. The call ended positively with agreement to schedule a tailored demo for Damien's engineering and security team.

examples.title: Aegis / Blackridge Investments - HIPAA Compliance Review
examples.summary: Aegis held a product demo call with Warren Beck (CISO) and Julia Tran (security analyst) from Blackridge, a financial services firm with a HIPAA-regulated healthcare subsidiary. The discussion centered on Aegis Comply v2, a newly released compliance reporting module, and how it could eliminate the manual, two-week evidence-gathering process Julia's team currently endures before audits. Key capabilities highlighted included on-demand and scheduled HIPAA reporting, multi-framework support (SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001), remediation guidance, and native LogVault integration. The call concluded with agreement to pursue a scoped proof of concept for the Blackridge Health Finance HIPAA environment, with Aegis to deliver a proposal by end of week.

examples.title: Aegis / Silverline Brands - Comply v2 Early Access Demo
examples.summary: Kevin O'Brien demoed Aegis Comply v2 to Dominic Flores of Silverline, with the call timed well ahead of Silverline's PCI DSS renewal audit in six weeks. The key highlights included on-demand compliance reporting (down to ~8 seconds), native support for PCI DSS, SOC 2, HIPAA, and ISO 27001 in one platform, and detailed gap analysis with remediation guidance. Dominic was highly enthusiastic, noting that this addresses real pain points including slow report generation, fragmented tooling, and difficulty communicating gaps to leadership. Next steps include scheduling a hands-on walkthrough with Silverline's compliance lead after the GA release on April 7th.

generated_label: backup / performance / response time / support response

examples.title: Support Case #6977 - Brightpath Commerce Slow Backup Performance
examples.summary: Kendra Wallace from a retail company contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding severely degraded backup performance, with nightly jobs taking 4-6 hours instead of the usual 90 minutes over the past three weeks. David Kim identified a likely root cause: Kendra's environment is running backup agent version 3.4.1, which has a known change block tracking issue that causes over-scanning, while a fix was released in version 3.6.0 in late January. David acknowledged a process gap in that Kendra was not notified about the update. The resolution plan includes upgrading to 3.6.0 and a follow-up call on Monday, with escalation to senior engineering promised if the issue persists.

examples.title: Support Case #6539 - Steelpoint Manufacturing LDAP Sync Failures
examples.summary: Harold Jensen, IT Director at Steelpoint Manufacturing, called regarding a 3-day-old unresolved LDAP sync failure with Aegis Identity affecting 200+ employees on a 24/7 manufacturing floor. Marcus Williams diagnosed the root cause as an SSL handshake failure caused by an internal CA-issued certificate renewed during a maintenance window on March 18th that was not trusted by the Aegis connector. The issue was resolved live on the call by uploading the internal CA root certificate into the Aegis Identity TLS Configuration settings. Harold expressed significant frustration over the slow initial support response and broader confidence concerns about Aegis following a prior Detect outage, and Marcus committed to escalating the response time failure and arranging a follow-up call with the account manager.

examples.title: Support Case #9856 - Silverline Brands Backup Window Exceeded
examples.summary: Dominic Flores, IT Director at Silverline Brands, called Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a two-week-old open case about backup jobs significantly overrunning their scheduled 1 AM–5 AM window, with SQL Server backups now taking up to 8–9 hours and causing production performance degradation. Support engineer David Kim identified a dramatic throughput drop from ~800 MB/s to ~180–220 MB/s, potentially linked to backend network routing changes made during a March remediation effort for a separate module (Aegis Detect). David initiated immediate workarounds including a manual agent update to version 4.2.0 and SQL job priority adjustments, and escalated the issue as P1 to the infrastructure team. A follow-up call was scheduled for the next morning at 10 AM Central with a Protect platform engineer to review root cause findings.

examples.title: Protect Team - Quarterly Planning
examples.summary: Mike Romano led a Q2 quarterly planning call for the Protect product with Ananya Sharma, Ravi Gupta, and Sofia Petrov. The team discussed three main initiatives: a 30%+ improvement to incremental backup performance targeting a late April canary rollout, a redesigned restore wizard UX that enables search-first file recovery, and multi-region replication which was scoped to Q3 rather than Q2 to avoid overpromising. The team also agreed to carve out capacity for tech debt work in the backup scheduler during Q2.

examples.title: Support Case #1750 - Vanta Health Systems SIEM Connector Issue
examples.summary: Vanta Health Systems contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a critical issue with their LogVault SIEM connector dropping event data since February 8th, including complete 45-minute to 1-hour blackout windows. Sarah Chen and Priya Patel identified the root cause as a known bug in connector version 3.4.1 where connection timeouts from F5 load balancer throttling cause the buffer to flush instead of retry. Immediate workarounds were provided (adjusting F5 connection limits and enabling the undocumented retry_on_timeout parameter), with a permanent fix available via upgrade to version 3.5.0. Dr. Cheng expressed significant frustration with the 10-day support response delay, undocumented parameters, and lack of proactive notification about the known bug in a healthcare/HIPAA environment.

examples.title: SOC 2 Audit Preparation - Internal
examples.summary: Raj Kapoor convened a meeting with Tyler, Ravi, Brian, and Mike to prepare for an upcoming SOC 2 Type II audit with a 90-day observation period starting March 1st. The team reviewed key control areas including access control, change management, incident tracking, monitoring, vendor management, and employee security training. Several gaps were identified, including a broken Zendesk-PagerDuty integration, inconsistent hotfix documentation, alert fatigue in the monitoring system, and incomplete security training completions. Action items were assigned to each team member with tight deadlines before the observation window opens.

examples.title: Identity Team - Q2 Roadmap
examples.summary: The team held a Q2 roadmap planning session for the Identity product, covering three major themes: JIT provisioning and SCIM improvements, adaptive MFA policies, and an Identity audit trail integrated with the Comply module. Technical leads discussed specific failure modes in SCIM sync, a redesigned attribute mapping UI with inline validation, and a phased approach to adaptive MFA starting with location and device posture signals. The session also touched on competitive pressure from SentinelShield, lingering customer trust concerns following a March Detect outage, and cross-team alignment with the Comply team around SOC 2 reporting gaps.

examples.title: Identity Team - Sprint Retro
examples.summary: The team held a sprint 22 retrospective covering the period of March 14-27 for the Identity team. The Detect outage on the 10th impacted sprint capacity, causing two tickets (MFA enforcement policy UI and SSO session timeout configuration) to slip into sprint 23. Completed work included SCIM provisioning bug fixes, SSO metadata refresh refactor, and partial IAM role hierarchy implementation. The team identified estimation issues with the SSO refactor, a test environment credential delay, and an upcoming Comply v2 integration as key concerns heading into sprint 23.

examples.title: Product Sync - Identity Roadmap
examples.summary: Ananya Sharma, Hannah Liu, and Nina Kowalski held a planning sync focused on the Aegis Identity Q2 roadmap. The team aligned on two primary workstreams: an MFA policy rules engine and an expanded SSO connector library, with the risk-signal/adaptive MFA layer pushed to Q3 due to scope and technical dependencies. They established a rough timeline targeting a May GA for the MFA rules engine and prioritized Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace as the first SSO connectors. Key process improvements were agreed upon, including locking specs two weeks before QA hand-off and including acceptance criteria upfront to enable parallel test case development.

examples.title: Aegis / Summit Trust - Platform Concerns Discussion
examples.summary: Maria Santos (CSM) met with Alicia Monroe from Summit Trust to address concerns ahead of their upcoming renewal. Alicia raised critical issues with Aegis Identity, including inconsistent MFA enforcement in their loan origination system and internal reporting portal, as well as SSO session timeouts occurring well before the configured 60-minute policy. Alicia also disclosed that her CISO has initiated exploratory conversations with competitor VaultEdge due to concerns that the IAM solution does not feel enterprise-grade, and brought up a six-hour Detect outage earlier in the month that damaged internal trust. Maria acknowledged the support escalation pattern as unacceptable and committed to several concrete follow-up actions to retain the account.

examples.title: Support Case #1514 - Meridian Capital Granular Restore Request
examples.summary: Jason Hoffman from Meridian contacted Aegis support (Sarah Chen) regarding the lack of granular file-level restore capabilities in Aegis Protect. An analyst had accidentally overwritten config files on an EBS-backed EC2 trading platform, requiring a two-hour manual workaround involving spinning up a temporary instance to retrieve the files. Sarah confirmed that file-level restore is not currently supported and documented a formal feature request covering granular file-level restore from EBS snapshots, restore-to-alternate-path functionality, and a file metadata preview capability. The case will be logged, Jason's colleague Marcus Webb will be added, and the CSM will be looped in for follow-up.

examples.title: Support Case #2638 - Pineridge Systems CloudPrime S3 Backup Connector
examples.summary: Priya from Aegis Cloud Security support and senior engineer Sarah Chen joined a call with Yuki Tanaka, Security Lead at Pineridge, to troubleshoot a failing CloudPrime S3 backup connector issue (Case 2638). Yuki reported that backup jobs were failing with a 403 error since the previous Thursday, despite no known IAM permission changes. Sarah identified two likely causes: a misconfigured connector endpoint (using the generic s3.amazonaws.com instead of the region-specific us-west-2 endpoint) and potentially restrictive CloudPrime Service Control Policies updated during a routine policy review around the same time. Yuki updated the endpoint setting live on the call and agreed to test the backup job, check SCP policies with their CloudPrime admin, and share CloudTrail logs with the Aegis team.

examples.title: Aegis / Coastal Living Co - Protect Module Expansion
examples.summary: Kevin O'Brien from Aegis spoke with Vanessa Cruz at Coastal Living about deploying Aegis Protect for their hybrid backup and recovery environment following a successful spring collection launch. Vanessa described a fragmented backup setup across CloudPrime and on-prem systems totaling 80-100TB across 40-50 workloads, with poor visibility being the primary pain point. Kevin outlined a phased three-part implementation plan, confirmed compatibility with their infrastructure, and discussed RPO/RTO requirements for their critical customer data platform. The call concluded with plans for a kickoff meeting the following Wednesday and next steps around onboarding documentation.

examples.title: Aegis / Coastal Living Co - Onboarding Kickoff
examples.summary: Maria Santos (CSM at Aegis Cloud Security) held a kickoff onboarding call with Vanessa Cruz from Coastal Living, who recently chose Aegis Protect over competitors CyberNova and VaultEdge primarily due to superior recovery time objectives. Vanessa outlined their infrastructure — a hybrid CloudPrime multi-region setup plus on-prem warehouse systems — and Maria walked through the 4-6 week three-phase onboarding process including environment discovery, backup policy configuration, and restore drills. Key topics included custom data retention policies for PCI DSS compliance, hybrid environment support, and the unified management console. The call ended with clear next steps: Maria to send a formal project plan, Vanessa to provision CloudPrime access and connect Maria with their DevOps engineer Raj Patel, and a technical discovery call scheduled for the following week.

examples.title: Aegis / Vanta Health Systems - Backup Strategy Workshop
examples.summary: Lisa Park and Aisha Johnson held an onboarding strategy workshop with Raymond Burke of Vanta Health to assess their backup and recovery environment and map out how Aegis Protect can address their needs. Raymond described a fragmented hybrid environment with ~450 on-prem VMs across two data centers and a growing CloudPrime footprint, including 200TB of DICOM imaging data. Key pain points include untested RTOs/RPOs, lack of unified backup visibility, VM coverage gaps, and complex HIPAA and tiered retention compliance requirements. The team aligned on a phased implementation approach starting with CloudPrime workloads, with next steps including an implementation plan, dashboard demo, and involvement of Vanta Health's infrastructure lead.
